text.title: Distribution Channel Research Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Distribution Channel Research methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Consider all 19 Traction channels (plus AI-era variants) before shortlisting; explicit rejection log for the 16 not picked.
rule.rationale: Founders default to whatever channel they already use; the explicit consideration log surfaces blind spots.

rule.statement: Shortlist exactly 3 channels for paid test; do not test more than 3 in parallel.
rule.rationale: Spreading <=$5k across 5+ channels yields no statistical signal anywhere.

rule.statement: Each channel test must have an explicit <=$500 stop-loss; abort the test the moment spend crosses the cap.
rule.rationale: Unbounded tests turn into 'we already spent it, let it run' sunk-cost spirals.

rule.statement: Score every test on 4 axes: CAC, reach, time-to-signal (days), retention-of-channel-customers (D30).
rule.rationale: Lowest-CAC channel often has worst retention; multi-axis scoring prevents single-metric optimization.

rule.statement: When locking the top 1-2 channels, name explicit tripwires (CAC > X, reach < Y) that trigger a retest.
rule.rationale: Channels degrade; without tripwires the team rides a dead channel.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
